title: Pass checkout auth to release installer subprocesses

sources.reason: Release validation must propagate checkout auth to installer subprocesses in private CI.
Regression coverage must confirm the checkout-auth env bridge and isolated Git command execution.

riskAssessment.reason: The fix changes how release validation reuses GitHub auth context without changing public release semantics.

executionDecision.reason: The checkout-auth env bridge is concrete and verifiable.

preReviewWarnings: Verify that no credential material is echoed into logs.
Verify installer subprocesses receive checkout auth only through Git environment variables.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: make check-release-distribution completes in a private repository CI context without losing checkout auth inside installer subprocesses.
The release distribution check still works in a public repository environment.
Regression tests cover checkout-auth env propagation and isolated Git command execution.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: The release installer subprocess does not inherit checkout auth in private GitHub CI, so the public release contract can fail when it clones the repository during installation.
constraints: Do not change public release validation semantics beyond safely reusing checkout auth for Git commands.
Keep the check reproducible in CI and locally.
Do not expose credential material in logs.
intent.rationale: Bridge checkout auth into child Git processes through environment variables so the installer can clone the tagged release in private CI without modifying the public release artifact.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
